data.title: Nasdaq futures higher boosted by Netflix's earnings beat
data.description:
Nasdaq and S&P 500 futures rose on Friday, while Dow futures fell, as Netflix's (NFLX) third quarter earnings beat helped bring cheer to investors.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.6% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 1 basis point to 4.09%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 3.96%.

Streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) rose 5% in premarket trade after it beat both its top and bottom line estimates in the third quarter. The firm also topped expectations for user growth.

Wall Street on Thursday scaled a fresh all-time intraday high on a boost from technology stocks and a robust retail sales report that bolstered expectations of a soft landing, but then gave up those gains in the afternoon and eventually ended largely flat.

"The main catalyst for the day's market moves was another batch of upbeat U.S. data, which dampened any immediate fears about a potential recession," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

U.S. data once again reminded investors of the wisdom of never underestimating the hedonism of the US consumer. September retail sales were strong, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"ECB yesterday for the first time this cycle. Meanwhile, markets priced out the likelihood of the Federal Reserve doing the same at every meeting over the next few months," Reid added.

The European Central Bank cut its policy rates by 25 basis points, as expected, on Thursday, its third cut of the year.

Housing starts and building permits unexpectedly slumped in September.

There are also a few Fed speakers, including Fed Atlanta President Raphael Bostic and Fed Minneapolis President Neel Kashkari.

data.title: Stocks to watch on Friday: AXP, PG and more
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Friday were mixed, as market participants parsed more quarterly results from high-profile names. Here are some stocks to watch on Friday:

  • Shares of tobacco companies were in focus ahead of the opening bell. According to a court-appointed mediator's proposal, cigarette makers Philip Morris International (PM), British American Tobacco (BTI) and Japan Tobacco (OTCPK:JAPAF)(OTCPK:JAPAY) will pay C$32.5B ($23.6B) as a settlement to end all tobacco product-related claims and litigation in Canada. The dispute stems from a 2015 ruling by a Quebec court against the Canadian units of the three cigarette giants that alleged the firms knew their products were causing cancer. Philip Morris (PM) was -0.5%, while U.S.-listed shares of British American (BTI) were -2.5%.
  • American Express (AXP) stock slipped nearly 3% in pre-market trading, after the credit card issuer slightly trimmed its full-year revenue growth guidance. The Dow 30 component now sees about 9% revenue growth in 2024, compared to an earlier forecast for growth of 9% to 11%. The company's Q3 revenue of $16.64B came in-line with estimates, while its discount revenue - the fee it earns from merchants for facilitating transactions - ticked up 4% Y/Y to $8.78B. See how AXP performed in charts.
  • Shares of Procter & Gamble (PG) shed nearly 1% ahead of market open, after the owner of popular consumer brands such as Gillette, Tide and Ariel reported a 1% Y/Y decrease in FQ1 2025 net sales. Its beauty segment and its baby, feminine and family care business - two units that usually put in a strong sales performance - struggled, partly due to volume declines in China. Still, top boss Jon Moeller said P&G (PG) was "on track to deliver within" its guidance ranges "on all key financial metrics for the fiscal year.” See how PG performed in charts.
  • SLB (SLB) stock dipped more than 1% in pre-market trading, after the company delivered a quarterly top-line miss and warned of cautious spending by some international producers. SLB (SLB), formerly known as Schlumberger, is the world's biggest oilfield services and equipment provider. SLB's (SLB) Q3 revenue of $9.16B comprised of a 12% Y/Y increase in international revenue to $7.43B and a much smaller 3% rise in North America revenue to $1.69B. Chief executive Olivier Le Peuch also noted "an environment where short-cycle activity growth softened." See how SLB performed in charts.

More on the markets

  • Gold And The Dow Both Trade At All-Time Highs: What History Says
  • S&P 500, Nasdaq 100 - Wall Street Indexes Rise As TSMC Leads Chip Stock Rally, Where To Next?
  • Dow notches 39th record close of 2024, while Nasdaq ekes out gains; S&P slips marginally
  • History says the stock market's bull run could keep going for years – Carson’s Detrick

data.title: Coherent slides as B. Riley downgrades on valuation concerns
data.description:
• Coherent (NYSE:COHR) shares slid more than 5% in premarket trading on Friday as B. Riley downgraded the semiconductor company, citing valuation concerns.
  • Coherent shares have gained 142% year-to-date, far stronger than the 24% rise in the Nasdaq, thanks to its position in the artificial intelligence and data center transceiver segment, analyst Dave Kang said. Kang lowered his rating to Neutral from Buy and kept his $86 price target.
  • The company has also benefited from its relationship with Nvidia (NVDA), as revenue from its 800G products jumped to more than $250M in the fourth-quarter, up from $150M in the first-half of 2024. And while 800G demand is likely to remain strong over the next two to three years, Nvidia may have added another supplier for 1.6T, believed to be China-based Eoptolink, Kang added.

data.title: CMS Energy, Avangrid win DoE awards to boost renewable energy
data.description:
CMS Energy (NYSE:CMS) and Avangrid (NYSE:AGR) said Friday they were awarded grants by the U.S. Department of Energy to deploy new technologies to help connect more renewable energy to the electric grids of Michigan and Maine, respectively.

Consumers Energy (CMS) said it was selected to receive a nearly $20M award to deploy new technology to help integrate electric vehicles into Michigan's electric grid, a commitment that will be matched by $20M from the company.

Consumers Energy (CMS) it will use the nearly $40M to add artificial intelligence-powered modules to 18K electric meters that are used by Michigan's EV owners, providing real-time data analytics to better understand the impact of the vehicles on the grid.

Avangrid (AGR) said its Central Maine Power subsidiary was awarded $31.8M in federal funding to deploy technologies to create a more modern and dynamic power grid to support the deployment of renewable energy in Maine.

Avangrid (AGR) said the latest grant brings Central Maine Power's total federal awards to nearly $500M in one year.

data.title: TSM under probe by US over potential dealings with China's Huawei - report
data.description:
The U.S. Commerce Department is probing if Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM) has been making AI or smartphone chips for Chinese company Huawei Technologies, and violated U.S. export rules, The Information reported.

Huawei was once in competition with Apple (AAPL) and Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) to be the world's biggest handset maker until U.S. restrictions, starting in 2019, began to curb its access to chip manufacturing tools needed to produce its most advanced models.

The U.S. has banned the Chinese tech giant from buying chips made by using American parts, due to national security concerns. The restrictions also curbs Huawei from using U.S. technology to make its own chips without a greenlight from the Commerce Department.

In recent weeks, the U.S. department has contacted TSM to ask if it was involved in manufacturing either smartphone chips or AI chips for Huawei, the report added.

The department is also checking if Huawei bought chips from TSM indirectly using intermediary companies with a different name to place orders on its behalf, the report noted.

The government body is also evaluating whether TSM carried out the required know-your-customer due diligence while accepting orders, according to the report.

TSM noted that it is committed to complying with all applicable rules and regulations, including applicable export controls. The Taiwan-based company added that it maintains an export system for monitoring and ensuring compliance, adding that it would take action to ensure compliance if there are potential issues, including conducting investigations and proactively communicating with relevant parties as required.

TSM — which produces chips for some of the world's largest tech companies, including Apple (AAPL), Nvidia and AMD — had previously signed a deal with the U.S. government to receive up to $6.6B in grants and as much as $5B in loans to boost domestic manufacturing of advanced semiconductors, under the U.S. CHIPS Act funding.

If the Commerce Department finds that TSM violated export rules in potential dealings with Huawei, it could impose a fine or more severe actions, such as temporarily halting the company's access to U.S. tech. Last year, the department fined Seagate Technology $300M for selling hard disk drive technology to Huawei, the report noted.

The probe's focus, partly, is also checking if TSM was involved in making AI chips designed by Huawei, the report added.

Huawei's AI server chips have seen rising demand in China as an alternate to Nvidia-made chips following export curbs by the U.S. on advanced chips by Nvidia and other American companies. In August, it was reported that Huawei was nearing to unveil a new chip to use for AI. Huawei had told potential clients that the new chip is comparable to Nvidia's H100, which was unveiled last year and is not directly available in China.

The U.S. department is also investigating whether TSM made smartphone chips for Huawei's phones. Prior to the U.S. ban Huawei was a big customer of TSM, according to the report.

Last year in August, Huawei surprised many by quietly launching its new flagship smartphone, Mate 60 Pro. The chip inside the phone ignited concerns in the U.S. and raised questions about how it was possible, without the company being able to access critical technologies. In April, Huawei unveiled its Pura 70 series, which features the Kirin 9010 chip, a follow-up to the Kirin 9000s reportedly made by Semiconductor Manufacturing International (OTCQX:SIUIF) for the Mate 60 Pro.

Huawei and its chip making partner Semiconductor Manufacturing International, reportedly, have been struggling with the production of the chips used in Huawei's next flagship phone Mate 70, due to the continuing impact of the U.S. ban.

data.title: River City Bank declares $0.37 dividend
data.description:
• River City Bank (OTCPK:RCBC) declares $0.37/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 0.53%
  • Payable Nov. 12; for shareholders of record Oct. 29; ex-div Oct. 29.
  • See RCBC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: HeartCore sees Q3 revenue between $17M and $19M
data.description:
• HeartCore Enterprises (NASDAQ:HTCR) on Friday said it expects revenues for the third quarter of 2024 to be between $17 million and $19 million, representing an increase of between 263% and 305%.
  • Consensus for Q3 revenue is $17.68M.
  • Revenues for the nine months ended September 30 are expected to be between $26 million and $28 million, representing an increase of between 40% and 51%.
  • Net income is expected to be between $9 million and $11 million for the third quarter of 2024, compared to a net loss of $2.5 million in the same period last year. Net income for the nine months ended September 30, 2024 is expected to be between $5 million and $7 million, compared to a net loss of $1.8 million in the same period last year.
  • HTCR +16.16% premarket to $1.15.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nokia to cut ~2k jobs in China, plans for EU workforce reduction - Reuters
A.I.S. Resources announces private placement financing
data.description:
• A.I.S. Resources (OTCPK:AISSF) announced a non-brokered private placement of up to 8.6M units at a  $0.035 per unit for gross proceeds of $301,000.
  • The company may pay finders fees of up to 8% cash and 8% finders warrants on a portion of the placement.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Housing starts, building permits unexpectedly slump in September
data.description:
• September Housing Starts: -0.5% M/M to 1.354M vs. 1.400M consensus and 1.361M prior (revised from 1.356M).
  • Building Permits: -2.9% M/M to 1.428M vs. 1.500M consensus and 1.470M prior (revised from 1.475M).
